device_description: The Huggler 2000 is a hypothetical neonatal comfort and positioning device intended to provide a gentle, controlled hugging sensation for infants in supervised clinical care settings. The device consists of a soft textile wrap, low-force pneumatic hug bladders, pressure sensors, temperature sensors, and a bedside control unit with preset comfort cycles and alarm limits. The system is designed to deliver low-level soothing compression while maintaining access for observation and routine care.
intended_purpose: The Huggler 2000 is intended to provide supervised, non-therapeutic comfort positioning and gentle calming pressure for babies in neonatal and pediatric care environments. It is intended for use by trained healthcare professionals according to facility protocols and is not intended to restrain, immobilize, or replace direct clinical monitoring.
indications_for_use: Short-duration comfort support for clinically stable infants under direct supervision
Adjunctive soothing support during non-invasive bedside care
Positioning support where gentle contact pressure is considered appropriate by the care team
contraindications: Infants with unstable respiratory status requiring unrestricted chest wall movement
Infants with fragile skin, open wounds, or pressure injury in the intended contact area
Use without direct clinical supervision or outside the specified weight range
target_patient_population: Clinically stable neonatal and pediatric patients within the labeled weight range for whom supervised comfort positioning is considered appropriate by the responsible clinician.
intended_user_profile: Trained neonatal nurses, pediatric nurses, and other healthcare professionals familiar with infant positioning, pressure-injury prevention, alarm response, and local monitoring procedures.
eu_mdr_classification_and_rule: EU Class IIa under EU MDR 2017/745, Rule 11
uk_mdr_classification_and_rule: UK Class IIa under UK MDR 2002 (as amended 2024), Rule 11
uk_responsible_person: Mastropietro Company UK Ltd, 44 Care Lane, London, UK
single_use_or_reusable: Reusable control unit with single-patient-use textile wrap
market_history: The Huggler 2000 was first placed on the EU market by Mastropietro Company in 2021. Post-market surveillance has focused on pressure control accuracy, textile integrity, alarm performance, cleaning compatibility, and user training for supervised neonatal comfort use. No change to intended purpose was made during the 2023 reporting period.

pmcf_plan_document.title: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up Plan for Huggler 2000
